tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Maka tatkala datang perintah Kami) yang memerintahkan supaya mereka dibinasakan (Kami selamatkan Saleh beserta orang-orang yang beriman bersama dia) jumlah mereka ada empat ribu orang (dengan rahmat dari Kami dan) Kami selamatkan pula mereka (dari kehinaan di hari itu) kalau dibaca yaumi-idzin dianggap sebagai isim yang mu`rab dan kalau dibaca yaumaidzin dianggap sebagai isim yang mabni karena diidhafatkan kepada isim yang mabni pula; demikianlah menurut pendapat kebanyakan ahli nahwu. (Sesungguhnya Rabbmu Dialah Yang Maha Kuasa lagi Maha Perkasa) Maha Menang

ar: وَأَخَذَ ٱلَّذِینَ ظَلَمُوا۟ ٱلصَّیۡحَةُ فَأَصۡبَحُوا۟ فِی دِیَـٰرِهِمۡ جَـٰثِمِینَ
ar_simple: وَأَخَذَ الَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا الصَّيْحَةُ فَأَصْبَحُوا فِي دِيَارِهِمْ جَاثِمِينَ
transliteration: wa-akhadha alladhīna ẓalamū l-ṣayḥatu fa-aṣbaḥū fī diyārihim jāthimīna

t.sahih-int: And the shriek seized those who had wronged, and they became within their homes [corpses] fallen prone
t.asad: And the blast [of God's punishment] overtook those who had been bent on evildoing: and then they lay lifeless, in their very homes, on the ground
t.yusuf-ali: The (mighty) Blast overtook the wrong-doers, and they lay prostrate in their homes before the morning
t.pickthall: And the (awful) Cry overtook those who did wrong, so that morning found them prostrate in their dwellings
t.arberry: And the evildoers were seized by the Cry, and morning found them in their habitations fallen prostrate
t.hilali-khan: And As-Saihah (torment - awful cry, etc.) overtook the wrong-doers, so they lay (dead), prostrate in their homes
t.itani: And the Scream struck those who transgressed, and they became motionless bodies in their homes.
t.mubarakpuri: And As-Sayhah (awful cry) overtook the wrongdoers, so they lay (dead), prostrate in their homes.
t.qarib: The blast [of God's punishment] overtook the evildoers, and they lay lifeless in their homes and on the ground
t.wahiduddin: The wrongdoers were overtaken by a dreadful blast and they lay dead in their homes
t.shakir: And the rumbling overtook those who were unjust, so they became motionless bodies in their abodes
t.haleem: The blast struck the evildoers and they lay dead in their homes
t.maariful: And those who transgressed were caught by the Cry, and they were found dead in their homes, fallen on their knees
t.khattab: And the ˹mighty˺ blast overtook the wrongdoers, so they fell lifeless in their homes
t.ghali: And the Shout took away (the ones) who did injustice, so (in the morning) they became cowering (bodies) in their residences
t.irving: The Blast caught those who had been doing wrong, so one morning they lay cowering in the homes
t.iwq: And the shriek seized those who had wronged, and they became within their homes corpses fallen prone

t_id.kemenag: Dan satu suara keras yang mengguntur menimpa orang-orang yang zalim itu, lalu mereka mati bergelimpangan di rumahnya,
t_id.sabiq: Satu suara keras yang disertai goncangan dan petir menimpa mereka karena mereka menzalimi diri dengan kekafiran dan permusuhan. Kemudian mereka binasa, mati bergelimpangan di tempat tinggal mereka dengan wajah tertelungkup, tanpa gerak sedikit pun.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Dan satu suara yang keras yang mengguntur menimpa orang-orang yang lalim itu lalu mereka mati bergelimpangan di tempat tinggal mereka) dalam keadaan bersimpuh pada lutut mereka padahal mereka telah mati semuanya.
t_id.iwq: Dan satu suara keras yang mengguntur menimpa orang-orang yang zalim itu, lalu mereka mati bergelimpangan di rumahnya,

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «وأخذ الذين ظلموا الصيحة فأصبحوا في ديارهم جاثمين» باركين على الركب ميِّتين
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَأَخَذَ ٱلَّذِينَ ظَلَمُواْ ٱلصَّيۡحَةُ فَأَصۡبَحُواْ فِي دِيَٰرِهِمۡ جَٰثِمِينَ (الصَّيْحَةُ: صَوْتٌ عَظِيمٌ مُهْلِكٌ مِنَ السَّمَاءِ, جَاثِمِينَ: هَامِدِينَ، سَاقِطِينَ عَلَى وُجُوهِهِمْ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Dan satu suara yang keras yang mengguntur menimpa orang-orang yang lalim itu lalu mereka mati bergelimpangan di tempat tinggal mereka) dalam keadaan bersimpuh pada lutut mereka padahal mereka telah mati semuanya

ar: كَأَن لَّمۡ یَغۡنَوۡا۟ فِیهَاۤۗ أَلَاۤ إِنَّ ثَمُودَا۟ كَفَرُوا۟ رَبَّهُمۡۗ أَلَا بُعۡدࣰا لِّثَمُودَ
ar_simple: كَأَنْ لَمْ يَغْنَوْا فِيهَا ۗ أَلَا إِنَّ ثَمُودَ كَفَرُوا رَبَّهُمْ ۗ أَلَا بُعْدًا لِثَمُودَ
transliteration: ka-an lam yaghnaw fīhā alā inna thamūdā kafarū rabbahum alā buʿ'dan lithamūda

t.sahih-int: As if they had never prospered therein. Unquestionably, Thamud denied their Lord; then, away with Thamud
t.asad: as though they had never lived there. Oh, verily, [the tribe of] Thamud denied their Sustainer! Oh, away with the Thamud
t.yusuf-ali: As if they had never dwelt and flourished there. Ah! Behold! for the Thamud rejected their Lord and Cherisher! Ah! Behold! removed (from sight) were the Thamud
t.pickthall: As though they had not dwelt there. Lo! Thamud disbelieved in their Lord. A far removal for Thamud
t.arberry: as if they never dwelt there: 'Surely Thamood disbelieved in their Lord, so away with Thamood
t.hilali-khan: As if they had never lived there. No doubt! Verily, Thamud disbelieved in their Lord. So away with Thamud
t.itani: As if they had never prospered therein. Indeed, Thamood rejected their Lord, so away with Thamood.
t.mubarakpuri: As if they had never lived there. No doubt! Verily, Thamud disbelieved in their Lord. So away with Thamud!
t.qarib: as though they had never lived there. Thamud denied their Lord, so away with Thamud
t.wahiduddin: as if they had never dwelt there. The Thamud denied their Lord; cursed are the tribe of Thamud
t.shakir: As though they had never dwelt in them; now surely did Samood disbelieve in their Lord; now surely, away with Samood
t.haleem: as though they had never lived and flourished there. Yes, the Thamud denied their Lord- so away with the Thamud
t.maariful: as though they had never lived there. Lo! The people of Thamūd disbelieved their Lord. Lo! Ruined were the people of Thamūd
t.khattab: as if they had never lived there. Surely Thamûd denied their Lord, so away with Thamûd
t.ghali: As if they had not flourished in it. Verily, Thamud surely disbelieved their Lord. Verily, away with Thamud
t.irving: It was just as if they had never prospered there. Had not Thamud disbelieved in their Lord? Was it not: "Away with Thamud
t.iwq: As if they had never prospered therein. Unquestionably, Thamud denied their Lord; then, away with Thamud

t_id.kemenag: seolah-olah mereka belum pernah berdiam di tempat itu. Ingatlah, sesungguhnya kaum Tsamud mengingkari Tuhan mereka. Ingatlah, kebinasaanlah bagi kaum Tsamud.
t_id.sabiq: Selesailah ihwal mereka dan hilanglah jejak mereka dari negeri itu, seakan-akan mereka tidak pernah tinggal di situ. Ihwal mereka menyiratkan sesuatu yang harus diperhatikan dan dijadikan pelajaran, bahwa kaum Tsamûd telah mengingkari tanda-tanda kekuasaan Tuhan, Sang Pencipta mereka. Oleh karena itu, mereka dihancurkan dan dijauhkan dari rahmat Allah.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Seolah-olah) lafal ka-an adalah takhfif daripada ka-anna sedangkan isimnya tidak disebutkan, kepanjangannya adalah ka-annahum, artinya seolah-olah mereka (belum pernah berdiam) belum pernah tinggal (di tempat itu) di dalam rumah mereka. (Ingatlah, sesungguhnya kaum Tsamud mengingkari Tuhan mereka. Ingatlah, kebinasaanlah bagi kaum Tsamud) lafal tsamuud dapat ditanwinkan sehingga menjadi tsamuudan dan dapat pula dibaca tanpa memakai tanwin sehingga menjadi tsamuuda. Makna yang dimaksud ialah kabilahnya.
t_id.iwq: seolah-olah mereka belum pernah berdiam di tempat itu. Ingatlah, sesungguhnya kaum Tsamud mengingkari Tuhan mereka. Ingatlah, kebinasaanlah bagi kaum Tsamud.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «كأن» مخففة واسمها محذوف أي كأنهم «لم يَغنوا» يقيموا «فيها» في دارهم «ألا إن ثمود كفروا ربهم ألا بُعدا لثمود» بالصرف وتركه على معنى الحي والقبيلة
tafsir.siraj-ar: كَأَن لَّمۡ يَغۡنَوۡاْ فِيهَآۗ أَلَآ إِنَّ ثَمُودَاْ كَفَرُواْ رَبَّهُمۡۗ أَلَا بُعۡدٗا لِّثَمُودَ (لَّمْ يَغْنَوْا: لَمْ يَعِيشُوا، وَيُقِيمُوا, بُعْدًا: هَلَاكًا، وَطَرْدًا)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Seolah-olah) lafal ka-an adalah takhfif daripada ka-anna sedangkan isimnya tidak disebutkan, kepanjangannya adalah ka-annahum, artinya seolah-olah mereka (belum pernah berdiam) belum pernah tinggal (di tempat itu) di dalam rumah mereka. (Ingatlah, sesungguhnya kaum Tsamud mengingkari Tuhan mereka. Ingatlah, kebinasaanlah bagi kaum Tsamud) lafal tsamuud dapat ditanwinkan sehingga menjadi tsamuudan dan dapat pula dibaca tanpa memakai tanwin sehingga menjadi tsamuuda. Makna yang dimaksud ialah kabilahnya

ar: وَلَقَدۡ جَاۤءَتۡ رُسُلُنَاۤ إِبۡرَ ٰهِیمَ بِٱلۡبُشۡرَىٰ قَالُوا۟ سَلَـٰمࣰاۖ قَالَ سَلَـٰمࣱۖ فَمَا لَبِثَ أَن جَاۤءَ بِعِجۡلٍ حَنِیذࣲ
ar_simple: وَلَقَدْ جَاءَتْ رُسُلُنَا إِبْرَاهِيمَ بِالْبُشْرَىٰ قَالُوا سَلَامًا ۖ قَالَ سَلَامٌ ۖ فَمَا لَبِثَ أَنْ جَاءَ بِعِجْلٍ حَنِيذٍ
transliteration: walaqad jāat rusulunā ib'rāhīma bil-bush'rā qālū salāman qāla salāmun famā labitha an jāa biʿij'lin ḥanīdhin

t.sahih-int: And certainly did Our messengers come to Abraham with good tidings; they said, "Peace." He said, "Peace," and did not delay in bringing [them] a roasted calf
t.asad: AND, INDEED, there came unto Abraham Our [heavenly] messengers, bearing a glad tiding. They bade him peace; [and] he answered, "[And upon you be] peace!"-and made haste to place before them a roasted calf
t.yusuf-ali: There came Our messengers to Abraham with glad tidings. They said, "Peace!" He answered, "Peace!" and hastened to entertain them with a roasted calf
t.pickthall: And Our messengers cam unto Abraham with good news. They said: Peace! He answered: Peace! and delayed not to bring a roasted calf
t.arberry: Our messengers came to Abraham with the good tidings; they said, 'Peace!' 'Peace,' he said; and presently he brought a roasted calf
t.hilali-khan: And verily, there came Our Messengers to Ibrahim (Abraham) with glad tidings. They said: Salam (greetings or peace!) He answered, Salam (greetings or peace!) and he hastened to entertain them with a roasted calf
t.itani: Our messengers came to Abraham with good news. They said, “Peace.” He said, “Peace.” Soon after, he came with a roasted calf.
t.mubarakpuri: And verily, there came Our messengers to Ibrahim with the glad tidings. They said: "Salaman (greetings or peace!)" He answered, "Salamun (greetings or peace!)," and he hastened to entertain them with a roasted calf.
t.qarib: To Abraham, Our [heavenly] Messengers brought good news. They said, "Peace," and he answered, "[And upon you be] peace." Without delay he placed a roasted calf before them
t.wahiduddin: Our messengers came to Abraham with good news and greeted him with, "Peace." He too said, "Peace be on you," and hastened to bring a roasted calf
t.shakir: And certainly Our apostles came to Ibrahim with good news. They said: Peace. Peace, said he, and he made no delay in bringing a roasted calf
t.haleem: To Abraham Our messengers brought good news. They said, ‘Peace.’ He answered, ‘Peace,’ and without delay he brought in a roasted calf
t.maariful: And Our emissaries came to Ibrāhīm with the good news. They greeted him with “Salām (peace).” He said, “Salām.” Then he took not much time in bringing a roasted calf
t.khattab: And surely Our messenger-angels came to Abraham with good news ˹of a son˺. They greeted ˹him with˺, “Peace!” And he replied, “Peace ˹be upon you˺!” Then it was not long before he brought ˹them˺ a ˹fat,˺ roasted calf
t.ghali: And indeed Our Messengers readily came to Ibrahim (Abraham) with the good tidings. They said, "Peace!" He said, "Peace!" Then in no way did he linger (i.e., He did not delay) to come with a fleshy well-roasted calf
t.irving: Our messengers came to Abraham with good news. They said: "Peace!"; he said: ´Peace!", and immediately set about bringing in a roast calf
t.iwq: And certainly did Our messengers come to Abraham with good tidings; they said, "Peace." He said, "Peace," and did not delay in bringing them a roasted calf

t_id.kemenag: Dan sesungguhnya utusan-utusan Kami (malaikat-malaikat) telah datang kepada lbrahim dengan membawa kabar gembira, mereka mengucapkan: "Selamat". Ibrahim menjawab: "Selamatlah," maka tidak lama kemudian Ibrahim menyuguhkan daging anak sapi yang dipanggang.
t_id.sabiq: Sesungguhnya Kami telah mengutus malaikat kepada Ibrâhîm untuk memberikan kabar gembira kepadanya dan istrinya tentang kelahiran seorang anak. Mereka memberikan hormat kepadanya dengan ucapan salam. Ibrâhîm menjawab penghormatan mereka dengan salam pula. Ia lalu cepat menyajikan jamuan berupa daging anak sapi yang dipanggang untuk dimakan.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Dan sesungguhnya utusan-utusan Kami telah datang kepada Ibrahim dengan membaca kabar gembira) yaitu akan diberi anak bernama Ishak dan menyusul Yakub anak Ishak sesudah itu. (mereka mengucapkan, "Selamat.") lafal salaaman menjadi mashdar (Ibrahim menjawab, "Selamatlah.") atas kalian (Maka tidak lama kemudian Ibrahim menyuguhkan daging anak sapi yang dipanggang) daging panggang anak sapi.
t_id.iwq: Dan sesungguhnya utusan-utusan Kami (malaikat-malaikat) telah datang kepada lbrahim dengan membawa kabar gembira, mereka mengucapkan: "Selamat". Ibrahim menjawab: "Selamatlah," maka tidak lama kemudian Ibrahim menyuguhkan daging anak sapi yang dipanggang.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «ولقد جاءت رسلنا إبراهيم بالبشرى» بإسحاق ويعقوب بعده «قالوا سلاما» مصدر «قال سلام» عليكم «فما لبث أن جاء بعجل حنيذِ» مشوي
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَلَقَدۡ جَآءَتۡ رُسُلُنَآ إِبۡرَٰهِيمَ بِٱلۡبُشۡرَىٰ قَالُواْ سَلَٰمٗاۖ قَالَ سَلَٰمٞۖ فَمَا لَبِثَ أَن جَآءَ بِعِجۡلٍ حَنِيذٖ (حَنِيذٍ: مَشْوِيٍّ بِالحِجَارَةِ المُحْمَاةِ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Dan sesungguhnya utusan-utusan Kami telah datang kepada Ibrahim dengan membaca kabar gembira) yaitu akan diberi anak bernama Ishak dan menyusul Yakub anak Ishak sesudah itu. (mereka mengucapkan, "Selamat.") lafal salaaman menjadi mashdar (Ibrahim menjawab, "Selamatlah.") atas kalian (Maka tidak lama kemudian Ibrahim menyuguhkan daging anak sapi yang dipanggang) daging panggang anak sapi

ar: فَلَمَّا رَءَاۤ أَیۡدِیَهُمۡ لَا تَصِلُ إِلَیۡهِ نَكِرَهُمۡ وَأَوۡجَسَ مِنۡهُمۡ خِیفَةࣰۚ قَالُوا۟ لَا تَخَفۡ إِنَّاۤ أُرۡسِلۡنَاۤ إِلَىٰ قَوۡمِ لُوطࣲ
ar_simple: فَلَمَّا رَأَىٰ أَيْدِيَهُمْ لَا تَصِلُ إِلَيْهِ نَكِرَهُمْ وَأَوْجَسَ مِنْهُمْ خِيفَةً ۚ قَالُوا لَا تَخَفْ إِنَّا أُرْسِلْنَا إِلَىٰ قَوْمِ لُوطٍ
transliteration: falammā raā aydiyahum lā taṣilu ilayhi nakirahum wa-awjasa min'hum khīfatan qālū lā takhaf innā ur'sil'nā ilā qawmi lūṭin

t.sahih-int: But when he saw their hands not reaching for it, he distrusted them and felt from them apprehension. They said, "Fear not. We have been sent to the people of Lot
t.asad: But when he saw that their hands did not reach out towards it, he deemed their conduct strange and became apprehensive of them. [But] they said: "Fear not! Behold, we are sent to the people of Lot
t.yusuf-ali: But when he saw their hands went not towards the (meal), he felt some mistrust of them, and conceived a fear of them. They said: "Fear not: We have been sent against the people of Lut
t.pickthall: And when he saw their hands reached not to it, he mistrusted them and conceived a fear of them. They said: Fear not! Lo! we are sent unto the folk of Lot
t.arberry: And when he saw their hands not reaching towards it, he was suspicious of them and conceived a fear of them. They said, 'Fear not; we have been sent to the people of Lot
t.hilali-khan: But when he saw their hands went not towards it (the meal), he felt some mistrust of them, and conceived a fear of them. They said: "Fear not, we have been sent against the people of Lout (Lot)
t.itani: But when he saw their hands not reaching towards it, he became suspicious of them, and conceived a fear of them. They said, “Do not fear, we were sent to the people of Lot.”
t.mubarakpuri: But when he saw their hands went not towards it (the meal), he mistrusted them, and conceived a fear of them. They said: "Fear not, we have been sent against the people of Lut."
t.qarib: When he saw that their hands did not reach out towards it, he found this strange and became apprehensive of them. They said, "Do not fear. We are sent against the people of Lot
t.wahiduddin: But when he saw that they made no move to eat, he found this strange and became afraid of them. They said, "Do not be afraid, for we have been sent to the people of Lot
t.shakir: But when he saw that their hands were not extended towards it, he deemed them strange and conceived fear of them. They said: Fear not, surely we are sent to Lut's people
t.haleem: When he saw that their hands did not reach towards the meal, he found this strange and became afraid of them. But they said, ‘Do not be afraid. We have been sent against the people of Lot.’
t.maariful: So, when he noticed their hands not acceding to it, he found them strange and felt scared of them. They said, “Be not scared. We have been sent to the people of LūT.”
t.khattab: And when he saw that their hands did not reach for the food, he became suspicious and fearful of them. They reassured ˹him˺, “Do not be afraid! We are ˹angels˺ sent ˹only˺ against the people of Lot.”
t.ghali: So, as soon as he saw their hands not getting to it, he disclaimed them and entertained a fright of them. They said, "Do not fear (anything); surely we have been sent to the people of Lut
t.irving: When he saw their hands did not reach out towards it, he felt uneasy with them and started to be afraid of them. They said: "Do not act afraid; we have been sent to Lot´s folk
t.iwq: But when he saw their hands not reaching for it, he distrusted them and felt from them apprehension. They said, "Fear not. We have been sent to the people of Lot

t_id.kemenag: Maka tatkala dilihatnya tangan mereka tidak menjamahnya, Ibrahim memandang aneh perbuatan mereka, dan merasa takut kepada mereka. Malaikat itu berkata: "Jangan kamu takut, sesungguhnya kami adalah (malaikat-ma]aikat) yang diutus kepada kaum Luth".
t_id.sabiq: Ketika melihat tangan mereka tidak menyentuh jamuan itu sedikit pun, seperti tamu-tamu pada umumnya, Ibrâhîm merasa bahwa mereka bukan tamu, tetapi malaikat. Ia menyembunyikan rasa takut kalau-kalau kedatangan mereka itu karena sesuatu yang dianggap salah oleh Allah atau untuk menyiksa kaumnya. Para malaikat itu berkata, setelah mengetahui rasa takut dalam diri Ibrâhîm, "Sesungguhnya kami diutus untuk membinasakan kaum Lûth."
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Maka tatkala dilihatnya tangan mereka tidak menjamahnya, Ibrahim memandang aneh perbuatan mereka) merasa heran dengan kelakuan mereka itu (dan mulai timbul) memendam rasa di dalam hati (rasa takut terhadap mereka) ketakutan. (Malaikat itu berkata, "Jangan kamu takut, sesungguhnya kami adalah malaikat-malaikat yang diutus kepada kaum Luth.") untuk membinasakan mereka.
t_id.iwq: Maka tatkala dilihatnya tangan mereka tidak menjamahnya, Ibrahim memandang aneh perbuatan mereka, dan merasa takut kepada mereka. Malaikat itu berkata: "Jangan kamu takut, sesungguhnya kami adalah (malaikat-ma]aikat) yang diutus kepada kaum Luth".

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «فلما رأى أيديهم لا تصل إليه نكرهم» بمعنى أنكرهم «وأوجس» أضمر في نفسه «منهم خيفة» خوفا «قالوا لا تخف إنا أرسلنا إلى قوم لوط» لنهلكهم
tafsir.siraj-ar: فَلَمَّا رَءَآ أَيۡدِيَهُمۡ لَا تَصِلُ إِلَيۡهِ نَكِرَهُمۡ وَأَوۡجَسَ مِنۡهُمۡ خِيفَةٗۚ قَالُواْ لَا تَخَفۡ إِنَّآ أُرۡسِلۡنَآ إِلَىٰ قَوۡمِ لُوطٖ (نَكِرَهُمْ: أَنْكَرَ ذَلِكَ مِنْهُمْ, وَأَوْجَسَ مِنْهُمْ خِيفَةً: أَحَسَّ فيِ نَفْسِهِ خَوْفًا مِنْهُمْ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Maka tatkala dilihatnya tangan mereka tidak menjamahnya, Ibrahim memandang aneh perbuatan mereka) merasa heran dengan kelakuan mereka itu (dan mulai timbul) memendam rasa di dalam hati (rasa takut terhadap mereka) ketakutan. (Malaikat itu berkata, "Jangan kamu takut, sesungguhnya kami adalah malaikat-malaikat yang diutus kepada kaum Luth.") untuk membinasakan mereka

ar: وَٱمۡرَأَتُهُۥ قَاۤئِمَةࣱ فَضَحِكَتۡ فَبَشَّرۡنَـٰهَا بِإِسۡحَـٰقَ وَمِن وَرَاۤءِ إِسۡحَـٰقَ یَعۡقُوبَ
ar_simple: وَامْرَأَتُهُ قَائِمَةٌ فَضَحِكَتْ فَبَشَّرْنَاهَا بِإِسْحَاقَ وَمِنْ وَرَاءِ إِسْحَاقَ يَعْقُوبَ
transliteration: wa-im'ra-atuhu qāimatun faḍaḥikat fabasharnāhā bi-is'ḥāqa wamin warāi is'ḥāqa yaʿqūba

t.sahih-int: And his Wife was standing, and she smiled. Then We gave her good tidings of Isaac and after Isaac, Jacob
t.asad: And his wife, standing [nearby], laughed [with happiness]; whereupon We gave her the glad tiding of [the birth of] Isaac and, after Isaac, of [his son] Jacob
t.yusuf-ali: And his wife was standing (there), and she laughed: But we gave her glad tidings of Isaac, and after him, of Jacob
t.pickthall: And his wife, standing by laughed when We gave her good tidings (of the birth) of Isaac, and, after Isaac, of Jacob
t.arberry: And his wife was standing by; she laughed, therefore We gave her the glad tidings of Isaac, and, after Isaac, of Jacob
t.hilali-khan: And his wife was standing (there), and she laughed (either, because the Messengers did not eat their food or for being glad for the destruction of the people of Lout (Lot). But We gave her glad tidings of Ishaque (Isaac), and after him, of Ya'qub (Jacob)
t.itani: His wife was standing by, so she laughed. And We gave her good news of Isaac; and after Isaac, Jacob.
t.mubarakpuri: And his wife was standing (there), and she laughed. But We gave her glad tidings of Ishaq, and after Ishaq, of Ya`qub.
t.qarib: His wife, standing [nearby], laughed [with happiness]. We gave her the good news of [the birth of] Isaac, and, after Isaac, of [Isaac's son] Jacob
t.wahiduddin: His wife, who was standing nearby, laughed when We gave her the good news of Isaac, and after Isaac, Jacob
t.shakir: And his wife was standing (by), so she laughed, then We gave her the good news of Ishaq and after Ishaq of (a son's son) Yaqoub
t.haleem: His wife was standing [nearby] and laughed. We gave her good news of Isaac and, after him, of Jacob
t.maariful: His wife was standing (nearby), and she laughed. Thus We gave her the good news about IsHāq, and about Ya‘qūb after IsHāq
t.khattab: And his wife was standing by, so she laughed, then We gave her good news of ˹the birth of˺ Isaac, and, after him, Jacob
t.ghali: And his wife was upright, so she laughed. Then We gave her the glad tidings of Ishaq, (Isaac) and even beyond Ishaq, of Yaaqub (Jacob)
t.irving: His wife was standing by and she laughed, so We informed her about Isaac, and following Isaac, Jacob
t.iwq: And his Wife was standing, and she smiled. Then We gave her good tidings of Isaac and after Isaac, Jacob

t_id.kemenag: Dan isterinya berdiri (dibalik tirai) lalu dia tersenyum, maka Kami sampaikan kepadanya berita gembira tentang (kelahiran) Ishak dan dari Ishak (akan lahir puteranya) Ya'qub.
t_id.sabiq: Saat itu istrinya berdiri ikut mendengarkan pembicaraan mereka di sebuah tempat tak jauh dari mereka. Ia kemudian tersenyum karena gembira mendengar Lûth, kemenakan suaminya, akan selamat. Melalui malaikat, Kami sampaikan kabar gembira bahwa ia, dari suaminya, akan melahirkan seorang putra yang bernama Ishâq. Anak itu nantinya akan hidup. Setelah itu, dari Ishâq, dia akan memperoleh seorang cucu yang bernama Ya'qûb.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Dan istrinya) yakni istri Nabi Ibrahim, yaitu Siti Sarah (berdiri) melayani mereka (lalu dia tersenyum) merasa gembira dengan akan dibinasakannya kaum Luth (maka Kami sampaikan kepadanya berita gembira tentang kelahiran Ishak dan menyusul sesudah) (Ishak Yakub) yaitu anak Nabi Ishak yang sempat melihat Nabi Ibrahim.
t_id.iwq: Dan isterinya berdiri (dibalik tirai) lalu dia tersenyum, maka Kami sampaikan kepadanya berita gembira tentang (kelahiran) Ishak dan dari Ishak (akan lahir puteranya) Ya'qub.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «وامرأته» أي امرأة إبراهيم سارة «قائمة» تخدمهم «فضحكت» استبشارا بهلاكهم «فبشرناها بإسحاق ومن وراء» بعد «إسحاق يعقوب» ولده تعيش إلى أن تراه
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَٱمۡرَأَتُهُۥ قَآئِمَةٞ فَضَحِكَتۡ فَبَشَّرۡنَٰهَا بِإِسۡحَٰقَ وَمِن وَرَآءِ إِسۡحَٰقَ يَعۡقُوبَ (وَمِن وَرَاءِ: وَمِنْ بَعْدِ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Dan istrinya) yakni istri Nabi Ibrahim, yaitu Siti Sarah (berdiri) melayani mereka (lalu dia tersenyum) merasa gembira dengan akan dibinasakannya kaum Luth (maka Kami sampaikan kepadanya berita gembira tentang kelahiran Ishak dan menyusul sesudah) (Ishak Yakub) yaitu anak Nabi Ishak yang sempat melihat Nabi Ibrahim

ar: قَالَتۡ یَـٰوَیۡلَتَىٰۤ ءَأَلِدُ وَأَنَا۠ عَجُوزࣱ وَهَـٰذَا بَعۡلِی شَیۡخًاۖ إِنَّ هَـٰذَا لَشَیۡءٌ عَجِیبࣱ
ar_simple: قَالَتْ يَا وَيْلَتَىٰ أَأَلِدُ وَأَنَا عَجُوزٌ وَهَٰذَا بَعْلِي شَيْخًا ۖ إِنَّ هَٰذَا لَشَيْءٌ عَجِيبٌ
transliteration: qālat yāwaylatā a-alidu wa-anā ʿajūzun wahādhā baʿlī shaykhan inna hādhā lashayon ʿajībun

t.sahih-int: She said, "Woe to me! Shall I give birth while I am an old woman and this, my husband, is an old man? Indeed, this is an amazing thing
t.asad: Said she: "Oh, woe is me! Shall I bear a child, now that I am an old woman and this husband of mine is an old man? Verily, that would be a strange thing indeed
t.yusuf-ali: She said: "Alas for me! shall I bear a child, seeing I am an old woman, and my husband here is an old man? That would indeed be a wonderful thing
t.pickthall: She said: Oh woe is me! Shall I bear a child when I am an old woman, and this my husband is an old man? Lo! this is a strange thing
t.arberry: She said, 'Woe is me! Shall I bear, being an old woman, and this my husband is an old man? This assuredly is a strange thing
t.hilali-khan: She said (in astonishment): "Woe unto me! Shall I bear a child while I am an old woman, and here is my husband, an old man? Verily! This is a strange thing
t.itani: She said, “Alas for me. Shall I give birth, when I am an old woman, and this, my husband, is an old man? This is truly a strange thing.”
t.mubarakpuri: She said (in astonishment): "Woe unto me! Shall I bear a child while I am an old woman, and here is my husband an old man Verily, this is a strange thing!"
t.qarib: She said, "Alas for me. How am I to bear a child when I am now an old woman, and this husband of mine is an old man? That would be an astonishing thing
t.wahiduddin: She said, "Alas! Shall I bear a child in this old age, while my husband here is also old? This is indeed a strange thing
t.shakir: She said: O wonder! shall I bear a son when I am an extremely old woman and this my husband an extremely old man? Most surely this is a wonderful thing
t.haleem: She said, ‘Alas for me! How am I to bear a child when I am an old woman, and my husband here is an old man? That would be a strange thing!’
t.maariful: She said, “Woe to me! Shall I give birth to a child while I am an old woman and this is my husband, an old man? This is something amazing indeed.”
t.khattab: She wondered, “Oh, my! How can I have a child in this old age, and my husband here is an old man? This is truly an astonishing thing!”
t.ghali: She said, "O woe to me! will I give birth and I am an old woman, and this my husband is an aged man? Surely, this is indeed a wonderful thing
t.irving: She said: "It´s too bad for me! Shall I bear a child while I am an old woman and this husband of mine is elderly? That would be an amazing thing
t.iwq: She said, "Woe to me! Shall I give birth while I am an old woman and this, my husband, is an old man? Indeed, this is an amazing thing

t_id.kemenag: Isterinya berkata: "Sungguh mengherankan, apakah aku akan melahirkan anak padahal aku adalah seorang perempuan tua, dan ini suamikupun dalam keadaan yang sudah tua pula?. Sesungguhnya ini benar-benar suatu yang sangat aneh".
t_id.sabiq: Istri Ibrâhîm berteriak heran, "Aneh! Mungkinkah aku akan melahirkan anak, padahal aku adalah perempuan tua dan suamiku kalian lihat juga sudah tua, sehingga tidak akan mungkin punya anak? Demi Allah, sungguh luar biasa apa yang aku dengar ini! Sebab bagaimana mungkin dua orang yang sudah tua seperti aku dan suamiku dapat menghasilkan anak?"
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Istrinya berkata, "Sungguh mengherankan) lafal wailataa ini merupakan kalimat yang biasa diucapkan di kala seseorang melihat perkara yang besar. Huruf ya yang ada padanya merupakan pergantian dari huruf alif (apakah aku akan melahirkan anak padahal aku adalah seorang perempuan tua) aku berumur sembilan puluh sembilan tahun (dan ini suamiku pun dalam keadaan yang sudah tua pula) Nabi Ibrahim pada saat itu berumur seratus atau seratus dua puluh tahun. Lafal syaikhan dinashabkan karena menjadi hal, sedangkan amilnya adalah isim isyarah yaitu lafal haadzaa tadi. (Sesungguhnya ini benar-benar suatu yang aneh") kedua pasangan yang sama-sama telah tua dapat mempunyai anak.
t_id.iwq: Isterinya berkata: "Sungguh mengherankan, apakah aku akan melahirkan anak padahal aku adalah seorang perempuan tua, dan ini suamikupun dalam keadaan yang sudah tua pula?. Sesungguhnya ini benar-benar suatu yang sangat aneh".

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «قالت يا ويلتى» كلمة تقال عند أمر عظيم والألف مبدلة من ياء الإضافة «أألد وأنا عجوز» لي تسع وتسعون سنة «وهذا بعلي شيخا» له مائة وعشرون نصبه على الحال والعامل فيه ما في ذا من الإشارة «إن هذا لشيء عجيب» أن يولد ولد لهرمين
tafsir.siraj-ar: قَالَتۡ يَٰوَيۡلَتَىٰٓ ءَأَلِدُ وَأَنَا۠ عَجُوزٞ وَهَٰذَا بَعۡلِي شَيۡخًاۖ إِنَّ هَٰذَا لَشَيۡءٌ عَجِيبٞ (يَا وَيْلَتَى: كَلِمَةُ تَعَجُّبٍ, بَعْلِي: زَوْجِي)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Istrinya berkata, "Sungguh mengherankan) lafal wailataa ini merupakan kalimat yang biasa diucapkan di kala seseorang melihat perkara yang besar. Huruf ya yang ada padanya merupakan pergantian dari huruf alif (apakah aku akan melahirkan anak padahal aku adalah seorang perempuan tua) aku berumur sembilan puluh sembilan tahun (dan ini suamiku pun dalam keadaan yang sudah tua pula) Nabi Ibrahim pada saat itu berumur seratus atau seratus dua puluh tahun. Lafal syaikhan dinashabkan karena menjadi hal, sedangkan amilnya adalah isim isyarah yaitu lafal haadzaa tadi. (Sesungguhnya ini benar-benar suatu yang aneh") kedua pasangan yang sama-sama telah tua dapat mempunyai anak

ar: قَالُوۤا۟ أَتَعۡجَبِینَ مِنۡ أَمۡرِ ٱللَّهِۖ رَحۡمَتُ ٱللَّهِ وَبَرَكَـٰتُهُۥ عَلَیۡكُمۡ أَهۡلَ ٱلۡبَیۡتِۚ إِنَّهُۥ حَمِیدࣱ مَّجِیدࣱ
ar_simple: قَالُوا أَتَعْجَبِينَ مِنْ أَمْرِ اللَّهِ ۖ رَحْمَتُ اللَّهِ وَبَرَكَاتُهُ عَلَيْكُمْ أَهْلَ الْبَيْتِ ۚ إِنَّهُ حَمِيدٌ مَجِيدٌ
transliteration: qālū ataʿjabīna min amri l-lahi raḥmatu l-lahi wabarakātuhu ʿalaykum ahla l-bayti innahu ḥamīdun majīdun

t.sahih-int: They said, "Are you amazed at the decree of Allah? May the mercy of Allah and His blessings be upon you, people of the house. Indeed, He is Praiseworthy and Honorable
t.asad: Answered [the messengers]: "Dost thou deem it strange that God should decree what He wills? The grace of God and His blessings be upon you, O people of this house! Verily, ever to be praised, sublime is He
t.yusuf-ali: They said: "Dost thou wonder at Allah's decree? The grace of Allah and His blessings on you, o ye people of the house! for He is indeed worthy of all praise, full of all glory
t.pickthall: They said: Wonderest thou at the commandment of Allah? The mercy of Allah and His blessings be upon you, O people of the house! Lo! He is Owner of Praise, Owner of Glory
t.arberry: They said, 'What, dost thou marvel at God's command? The mercy of God and His blessings be upon you, O people of the House! Surely He is All-laudable, All-glorious
t.hilali-khan: They said: "Do you wonder at the Decree of Allah? The Mercy of Allah and His Blessings be on you, O the family [of Ibrahim (Abraham)]. Surely, He (Allah) is All-Praiseworthy, All-Glorious
t.itani: They said, “Do you marvel at the decree of God? The mercy and blessings of God are upon you, O people of the house. He is Praiseworthy and Glorious.”
t.mubarakpuri: They said: "Do you wonder at the decree of Allah The mercy of Allah and His blessings be on you, O the family [of Ibrahim]. Surely, He (Allah) is All-Praiseworthy, All-Glorious."
t.qarib: [The Messengers] said, "Are you astonished at God's command? May the mercy of God and His blessing be upon you, people of this house. For He is worthy of all praise and Glory
t.wahiduddin: They said, "Are you astonished at God's command? May the mercy of God and His blessings be upon you, O people of this house. Surely, He is praiseworthy and glorious
t.shakir: They said: Do you wonder at Allah's bidding? The mercy of Allah and His blessings are on you, O people of the house, surely He is Praised, Glorious
t.haleem: They said, ‘Are you astonished at what God ordains? The grace of God and His blessings be upon you, people of this house! For He is worthy of all praise and glory.’
t.maariful: They said, “Do you marvel at Allah’s command? Allah’s mercy and His blessings have always been upon you, O people of the house. Surely, He is Praiseworthy, Glorious.”
t.khattab: They responded, “Are you astonished by Allah’s decree? May Allah’s mercy and blessings be upon you, O people of this house. Indeed, He is Praiseworthy, All-Glorious.”
t.ghali: They said, "Do you wonder at the Command of Allah? The mercy of Allah and His blessings be upon you, O population (1) of the Home! Surely He is Ever-Praiseworthy, Ever-Glorious
t.irving: They said: "Do you marvel at God´s command? God´s mercy and blessings are on all you people in [this] house. He is Praiseworthy, Glorious
t.iwq: And they said, "Are you amazed at the decree of Allah? May the mercy of Allah and His blessings be upon you, people of the house. Indeed, He is Praiseworthy and Honorable

t_id.kemenag: Para malaikat itu berkata: "Apakah kamu merasa heran tentang ketetapan Allah? (Itu adalah) rahmat Allah dan keberkatan-Nya, dicurahkan atas kamu, hai ahlulbait! Sesungguhnya Allah Maha Terpuji lagi Maha Pemurah".
t_id.sabiq: Malaikat berkata kepadanya, "Apakah kamu merasa heran akan mendapatkan anak meskipun kalian berdua telah berusia tua, padahal itu semua terjadi atas perintah Allah yang tidak dapat dihalangai oleh sesuatu apapun? Itulah rahmat dan nikmat Allah yang diberikan kepada kalian, sanak keluarga Nabi. Bukanlah suatu hal yang aneh jika Allah memberikan kepada kalian sesuatu yang tidak pernah diberikan kepada orang lain. Dialah yang melakukan segala sesuatu yang patut dipuji. Dia Mahaagung, serta banyak memberikan kebaikan, kemuliaan dan karunia.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Para malaikat itu berkata, "Apakah kamu merasa heran tentang perintah Allah) yakni kekuasaan-Nya (itu adalah rahmat Allah dan keberkatan-Nya dicurahkan atas kalian) hai (ahlul bait) keluarga Nabi Ibrahim. (Sesungguhnya Allah Maha Terpuji) sangat terpuji (lagi Maha Pemurah") Maha Pengasih.
t_id.iwq: Para malaikat itu berkata: "Apakah kamu merasa heran tentang ketetapan Allah? (Itu adalah) rahmat Allah dan keberkatan-Nya, dicurahkan atas kamu, hai ahlulbait! Sesungguhnya Allah Maha Terpuji lagi Maha Pemurah".

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «قالوا أتعجبين من أمر الله» قدرته «رحمة الله وبركاته عليكم» يا «أهل البيت» بيت إبراهيم «إنه حميد» محمود «مجيد» كريم
tafsir.siraj-ar: قَالُوٓاْ أَتَعۡجَبِينَ مِنۡ أَمۡرِ ٱللَّهِۖ رَحۡمَتُ ٱللَّهِ وَبَرَكَٰتُهُۥ عَلَيۡكُمۡ أَهۡلَ ٱلۡبَيۡتِۚ إِنَّهُۥ حَمِيدٞ مَّجِيدٞ (حَمِيدٌ: مَحْمُودُ الصِّفَاتِ وَالأَفْعَالِ, مَّجِيدٌ: ذُو عَظَمَةٍ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Para malaikat itu berkata, "Apakah kamu merasa heran tentang perintah Allah) yakni kekuasaan-Nya (itu adalah rahmat Allah dan keberkatan-Nya dicurahkan atas kalian) hai (ahlul bait) keluarga Nabi Ibrahim. (Sesungguhnya Allah Maha Terpuji) sangat terpuji (lagi Maha Pemurah") Maha Pengasih

ar: فَلَمَّا ذَهَبَ عَنۡ إِبۡرَ ٰهِیمَ ٱلرَّوۡعُ وَجَاۤءَتۡهُ ٱلۡبُشۡرَىٰ یُجَـٰدِلُنَا فِی قَوۡمِ لُوطٍ
ar_simple: فَلَمَّا ذَهَبَ عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ الرَّوْعُ وَجَاءَتْهُ الْبُشْرَىٰ يُجَادِلُنَا فِي قَوْمِ لُوطٍ
transliteration: falammā dhahaba ʿan ib'rāhīma l-rawʿu wajāathu l-bush'rā yujādilunā fī qawmi lūṭin

t.sahih-int: And when the fright had left Abraham and the good tidings had reached him, he began to argue with Us concerning the people of Lot
t.asad: And when the fear had left Abraham, and the glad tiding had been conveyed to him, he began to plead with Us for Lot's people
t.yusuf-ali: When fear had passed from (the mind of) Abraham and the glad tidings had reached him, he began to plead with us for Lut's people
t.pickthall: And when the awe departed from Abraham, and the glad news reached him, he pleaded with Us on behalf of the folk of Lot
t.arberry: So, when the awe departed from Abraham and the good tidings came to him, he was disputing with Us concerning the people of Lot
t.hilali-khan: Then when the fear had gone away from (the mind of) Ibrahim (Abraham), and the glad tidings had reached him, he began to plead with Us (Our Messengers) for the people of Lout (Lot)
t.itani: When Abraham's fear subsided, and the good news had reached him, he started pleading with Us concerning the people of Lot.
t.mubarakpuri: Then when the fear had gone away from (the mind of) Ibrahim, and the glad tidings had reached him, he began to plead with Us (Our messengers) for the people of Lut.
t.qarib: When the fear left Abraham and the good news had been conveyed to him, he began to plead with Us for Lot's people
t.wahiduddin: When the fear had left Abraham, and the glad tidings had been conveyed to him, he began to plead with Us for Lot's people
t.shakir: So when fear had gone away from Ibrahim and good news came to him, he began to plead with Us for Lut's people
t.haleem: Then, when the fear left Abraham and the good news came to him, he pleaded with Us for Lot’s people
t.maariful: So, when fear left Ibrāhīm and the happy news came to him, he started disputing with Us for the people of LūT
t.khattab: Then after the fear had left Abraham, and the good news had reached him, he began to plead with Us for the people of Lot
t.ghali: So, as soon as dread had gone away from Ibrahim (Abraham) and the good tidings came to him, he was disputing with Us concerning the people of Lut (Lot)
t.irving: So when his dismay left Abraham and the news came (home) to him, he pleaded with Us about Lot´s folk
t.iwq: And when the fright had left Abraham and the good tidings had reached him, he began to argue with Us concerning the people of Lot

t_id.kemenag: Maka tatkala rasa takut hilang dari Ibrahim dan berita gembira telah datang kepadanya, diapun bersoal jawab dengan (malaikat-malaikat) Kami tentang kaum Luth.
t_id.sabiq: Ketika rasa takut telah hilang dari dalam diri Ibrâhîm dan ia mendengar berita gembira akan kedatangan seorang putra, rasa ibanya pun muncul. Kemudian ia mendebat para melaikat tentang rencana pemusnahan kaum Lûth.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Maka tatkala rasa takut hilang dari Ibrahim) perasaan takut telah lenyap dari hatinya (dan berita gembira telah datang kepadanya) yaitu memperoleh anak, mulailah (dia pun bersoal jawab dengan Kami) yakni dengan malaikat-malaikat utusan Kami (tentang) mengenai perihal (kaum Luth).
t_id.iwq: Maka tatkala rasa takut hilang dari Ibrahim dan berita gembira telah datang kepadanya, diapun bersoal jawab dengan (malaikat-malaikat) Kami tentang kaum Luth.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «فلما ذهب عن إبراهيم الروع» الخوف «وجاءته البشرى» بالولد أخذ «يجادلنا» يجادل رسلنا «في» شأن «قوم لوط»
tafsir.siraj-ar: فَلَمَّا ذَهَبَ عَنۡ إِبۡرَٰهِيمَ ٱلرَّوۡعُ وَجَآءَتۡهُ ٱلۡبُشۡرَىٰ يُجَٰدِلُنَا فِي قَوۡمِ لُوطٍ (الرَّوْعُ: الخَوْفُ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Maka tatkala rasa takut hilang dari Ibrahim) perasaan takut telah lenyap dari hatinya (dan berita gembira telah datang kepadanya) yaitu memperoleh anak, mulailah (dia pun bersoal jawab dengan Kami) yakni dengan malaikat-malaikat utusan Kami (tentang) mengenai perihal (kaum Luth)

ar: إِنَّ إِبۡرَ ٰهِیمَ لَحَلِیمٌ أَوَّ ٰهࣱ مُّنِیبࣱ
ar_simple: إِنَّ إِبْرَاهِيمَ لَحَلِيمٌ أَوَّاهٌ مُنِيبٌ
transliteration: inna ib'rāhīma laḥalīmun awwāhun munībun

t.sahih-int: Indeed, Abraham was forbearing, grieving and [frequently] returning [to Allah]
t.asad: for, behold, Abraham was most clement, most tender-hearted, intent upon turning to God again and again
t.yusuf-ali: For Abraham was, without doubt, forbearing (of faults), compassionate, and given to look to Allah
t.pickthall: Lo! Abraham was mild, imploring, penitent
t.arberry: Abraham was clement, compassionate, penitent
t.hilali-khan: Verily, Ibrahim (Abraham) was, without doubt, forbearing, used to invoke Allah with humility, and was repentant (to Allah all the time, again and again)
t.itani: Abraham was gentle, kind, penitent.
t.mubarakpuri: Verily, Ibrahim was, without doubt, forbearing, used to invoke Allah with humility, and was repentant.
t.qarib: Abraham was forbearing, tender-hearted, and devout
t.wahiduddin: for Abraham was forbearing, tender-hearted and oft-returning to God
t.shakir: Most surely Ibrahim was forbearing, tender-hearted, oft-returning (to Allah)
t.haleem: for Abraham was forbearing, tender-hearted, and devout
t.maariful: Surely, Ibrāhīm is forbearing, very penitent, ever-turning to Allah
t.khattab: Truly, Abraham was forbearing, tender-hearted, and ever turning ˹to his Lord˺
t.ghali: Surely Ibrahim is indeed constantly forbearing, most plaintive, constantly penitent
t.irving: Abraham was so lenient, worried, concerned
t.iwq: Indeed, Abraham was forbearing, grieving and frequently returning to Allah

t_id.kemenag: Sesungguhnya Ibrahim itu benar-benar seorang yang penyantun lagi penghiba dan suka kembali kepada Allah.
t_id.sabiq: Ibrâhîm adalah seorang yang sangat penyabar. Ia tidak menyukai suatu azab disegerakan, sering merasa iba melihat orang lain tertimpa musibah dan selalu bertobat dengan melakukan sesuatu yang disukai dan diridai oleh Allah. Kelembutan dan kasih sayangnya itulah yang mendorongnya untuk mengajukan keberatan kepada para malaikat dengan harapan kaum Nabi Lûth tidak ditimpa azab Allah dan mereka dapat bertobat kepada-Nya.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Sesungguhnya Ibrahim itu benar-benar penyantun) sangat sabar (lagi penghiba dan suka kembali kepada Allah) banyak istighfarnya atau ia banyak mengucapkan kalimat innaa lillaahi wa innaa ilaihi raaji`uuna. Maka Nabi Ibrahim berkata kepada para malaikat itu, "Apakah kalian akan membinasakan suatu kota yang di dalamnya terdapat tiga ratus orang beriman?" Para malaikat menjawab, "Tidak." Ia kembali bertanya, "Apakah kalian akan membinasakan suatu kota yang di dalamnya terdapat dua ratus orang yang beriman?" Para malaikat menjawab, "Tidak." Ia bertanya lagi, "Apakah kalian akan membinasakan suatu kota yang di dalamnya terdapat empat puluh orang yang beriman." Para malaikat menjawab, "Tidak." Ia kembali bertanya, "Apakah kalian akan membinasakan suatu kota yang di dalamnya terdapat empat belas orang yang beriman?" Mereka menjawab, "Tidak." Nabi Ibrahim kembali bertanya, "Bagaimana pendapat kalian jika di dalam sebuah kota terdapat hanya seorang yang beriman?" Mereka menjawab, "Tidak pula." Maka Nabi Ibrahim berkata kepada mereka, "Sesungguhnya di dalam kota tersebut terdapat Nabi Luth." Mereka menjawab, "Kami lebih mengetahui tentang orang-orang beriman yang terdapat di dalamnya
t_id.iwq: Sesungguhnya Ibrahim itu benar-benar seorang yang penyantun lagi penghiba dan suka kembali kepada Allah.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «إن إبراهيم لحليم» كثير الأناة «أوَّاهٌ مُنيب» رجّاع، فقال لهم أتهلكون قرية فيها ثلاثمائة مؤمن؟ قالوا لا، أفتهلكون قرية فيها مائتا مؤمن؟ قالوا لا، قال أفتهلكون قرية فيها أربعون مؤمنا؟ قالوا لا، قال أفتهلكون قرية فيها أربعة عشر مؤمنا؟ قالوا لا، قال أفرأيتم إن كان فيها مؤمن واحد قالوا لا، إن فيها لوطا قالوا نحن أعلم بمن فيها إلخ
tafsir.siraj-ar: إِنَّ إِبۡرَٰهِيمَ لَحَلِيمٌ أَوَّـٰهٞ مُّنِيبٞ (أَوَّاهٌ: كَثِيرُ التَّضَرُّعِ وَالدُّعَاءِ, مُّنِيبٌ: تَائِبٌ يَرْجِعُ إِلَى اللهِ فِي أُمُورِهِ كُلِّهَا)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Sesungguhnya Ibrahim itu benar-benar penyantun) sangat sabar (lagi penghiba dan suka kembali kepada Allah) banyak istighfarnya atau ia banyak mengucapkan kalimat innaa lillaahi wa innaa ilaihi raaji`uuna. Maka Nabi Ibrahim berkata kepada para malaikat itu, "Apakah kalian akan membinasakan suatu kota yang di dalamnya terdapat tiga ratus orang beriman?" Para malaikat menjawab, "Tidak." Ia kembali bertanya, "Apakah kalian akan membinasakan suatu kota yang di dalamnya terdapat dua ratus orang yang beriman?" Para malaikat menjawab, "Tidak." Ia bertanya lagi, "Apakah kalian akan membinasakan suatu kota yang di dalamnya terdapat empat puluh orang yang beriman." Para malaikat menjawab, "Tidak." Ia kembali bertanya, "Apakah kalian akan membinasakan suatu kota yang di dalamnya terdapat empat belas orang yang beriman?" Mereka menjawab, "Tidak." Nabi Ibrahim kembali bertanya, "Bagaimana pendapat kalian jika di dalam sebuah kota terdapat hanya seorang yang beriman?" Mereka menjawab, "Tidak pula." Maka Nabi Ibrahim berkata kepada mereka, "Sesungguhnya di dalam kota tersebut terdapat Nabi Luth." Mereka menjawab, "Kami lebih mengetahui tentang orang-orang beriman yang terdapat di dalamnya

ar: یَـٰۤإِبۡرَ ٰهِیمُ أَعۡرِضۡ عَنۡ هَـٰذَاۤۖ إِنَّهُۥ قَدۡ جَاۤءَ أَمۡرُ رَبِّكَۖ وَإِنَّهُمۡ ءَاتِیهِمۡ عَذَابٌ غَیۡرُ مَرۡدُودࣲ
ar_simple: يَا إِبْرَاهِيمُ أَعْرِضْ عَنْ هَٰذَا ۖ إِنَّهُ قَدْ جَاءَ أَمْرُ رَبِّكَ ۖ وَإِنَّهُمْ آتِيهِمْ عَذَابٌ غَيْرُ مَرْدُودٍ
transliteration: yāib'rāhīmu aʿriḍ ʿan hādhā innahu qad jāa amru rabbika wa-innahum ātīhim ʿadhābun ghayru mardūdin

t.sahih-int: [The angels said], "O Abraham, give up this [plea]. Indeed, the command of your Lord has come, and indeed, there will reach them a punishment that cannot be repelled
t.asad: [But God's messengers replied:] "O Abraham! Desist from this [pleading]! Behold, thy Sustainer's judgment has already gone forth: and, verily, there shall fall upon them a chastisement which none can avert
t.yusuf-ali: O Abraham! Seek not this. The decree of thy Lord hath gone forth: for them there cometh a penalty that cannot be turned back
t.pickthall: (It was said) O Abraham! Forsake this! Lo! thy Lord's commandment hath gone forth, and lo! there cometh unto them a doom which cannot be repelled
t.arberry: O Abraham, turn away from this; thy Lord's command has surely come, and there is coming upon them a chastisement not to be turned back
t.hilali-khan: O Ibrahim (Abraham)! Forsake this. Indeed, the Commandment of your Lord has gone forth. Verily, there will come a torment for them which cannot be turned back
t.itani: “O Abraham, refrain from this. The command of your Lord has come; they have incurred an irreversible punishment.”
t.mubarakpuri: "O Ibrahim! Forsake this. Indeed, the commandment of your Lord has gone forth. Verily, there will come a torment for them which cannot be turned back."
t.qarib: [God's Messengers replied,] "Abraham, stop this [pleading]. Your Lord's judgment has already passed. Punishment is coming to them, which cannot be turned back
t.wahiduddin: We said, "Abraham, cease your pleading: the command of your Lord has gone forth. There shall fall upon them a punishment which none can avert
t.shakir: O Ibrahim! leave off this, surely the decree of your Lord has come to pass, and surely there must come to them a chastisement that cannot be averted
t.haleem: ‘Abraham, cease your pleading: what your Lord has ordained has come about; punishment is coming to them, which cannot be turned back.’
t.maariful: “O Ibrāhīm! Leave it. In fact, the command of your Lord has come to pass, and there has to come upon them a punishment that cannot be averted.”
t.khattab: ˹The angels said,˺ “O Abraham! Plead no more! Your Lord’s decree has already come, and they will certainly be afflicted with a punishment that cannot be averted!”
t.ghali: O Ibrahim, veer away from this. Surely your Lord's Command has already come; and surely there is coming up to them a torment not to be turned back." (i.e., without any possibility of being turned back)
t.irving: Abraham, avoid this! It is merely that your Lord´s command has come, and torment which cannot be averted has been brought them
t.iwq: The angels said, "O Abraham, give up this plea. Indeed, the command of your Lord has come, and indeed, there will reach them a punishment that cannot be repelled

t_id.kemenag: Hai Ibrahim, tinggalkanlah soal jawab ini, sesungguhnya telah datang ketetapan Tuhanmu, dan sesungguhnya mereka itu akan didatangi azab yang tidak dapat ditolak.
t_id.sabiq: Malaikat berkata, "Hai Ibrâhîm, janganlah kamu mengajukan keberatan dan memohon belas kasih untuk mereka. Perintah untuk menghancurkan mereka telah datang dari Tuhan. Diprotes atau tidak, mereka akan tetap didatangi azab yang tidak dapat ditolak."
t_id.jalalayn-id: Maka tatkala Nabi Ibrahim berkepanjangan di dalam bertanya jawab dengan mereka, lalu mereka mengatakan: ("Hai Ibrahim! Tinggalkanlah soal jawab ini) perbantahan ini (sesungguhnya telah datang ketetapan Rabbmu) untuk membinasakan mereka (dan sesungguhnya mereka itu akan didatangi azab yang tidak dapat ditolak.")
t_id.iwq: Hai Ibrahim, tinggalkanlah soal jawab ini, sesungguhnya telah datang ketetapan Tuhanmu, dan sesungguhnya mereka itu akan didatangi azab yang tidak dapat ditolak.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: فلما أطال مجادلتهم قالوا: «يا إبراهيم أعرض عن هذا» الجدال «إنه قد جاء أمر ربك» بهلاكهم «وإنهم آتيهم عذاب غير مردود»
tafsir.siraj-ar: يَـٰٓإِبۡرَٰهِيمُ أَعۡرِضۡ عَنۡ هَٰذَآۖ إِنَّهُۥ قَدۡ جَآءَ أَمۡرُ رَبِّكَۖ وَإِنَّهُمۡ ءَاتِيهِمۡ عَذَابٌ غَيۡرُ مَرۡدُودٖ
tafsir.jalalayn-id: Maka tatkala Nabi Ibrahim berkepanjangan di dalam bertanya jawab dengan mereka, lalu mereka mengatakan: ("Hai Ibrahim! Tinggalkanlah soal jawab ini) perbantahan ini (sesungguhnya telah datang ketetapan Rabbmu) untuk membinasakan mereka (dan sesungguhnya mereka itu akan didatangi azab yang tidak dapat ditolak)

ar: وَلَمَّا جَاۤءَتۡ رُسُلُنَا لُوطࣰا سِیۤءَ بِهِمۡ وَضَاقَ بِهِمۡ ذَرۡعࣰا وَقَالَ هَـٰذَا یَوۡمٌ عَصِیبࣱ
ar_simple: وَلَمَّا جَاءَتْ رُسُلُنَا لُوطًا سِيءَ بِهِمْ وَضَاقَ بِهِمْ ذَرْعًا وَقَالَ هَٰذَا يَوْمٌ عَصِيبٌ
transliteration: walammā jāat rusulunā lūṭan sīa bihim waḍāqa bihim dharʿan waqāla hādhā yawmun ʿaṣībun

t.sahih-int: And when Our messengers, [the angels], came to Lot, he was anguished for them and felt for them great discomfort and said, "This is a trying day
t.asad: AND WHEN Our messengers came unto Lot, he was sorely grieved on their account, seeing that it was beyond his power to shield them; and he exclaimed: "This is a woeful day
t.yusuf-ali: When Our messengers came to Lut, he was grieved on their account and felt himself powerless (to protect) them. He said: "This is a distressful day
t.pickthall: And when Our messengers came unto Lot, he was distressed and knew not how to protect them. He said: This is a distressful day
t.arberry: And when Our messengers came to Lot, he was troubled on their account and distressed for them, and he said, 'This is a fierce day
t.hilali-khan: And when Our Messengers came to Lout (Lot), he was grieved on their account and felt himself straitened for them (lest the town people should approach them to commit sodomy with them). He said: "This is a distressful day
t.itani: And when Our envoys came to Lot, he was anxious for them, and concerned for them. He said, “This is a dreadful day.”
t.mubarakpuri: And when Our messengers came to Lut, he was grieved on account of them and was concerned for them. He said: "This is a distressful day."
t.qarib: When Our Messengers came to Lot, he was sad for [his people], seeing that it was beyond his power to protect them, and he said, "This is a truly terrible day
t.wahiduddin: When Our messengers came to Lot, he was uneasy on this account and felt powerless to protect them. He said, "This is a dreadful day
t.shakir: And when Our apostles came to Lut, he was grieved for them, and he lacked strength to protect them, and said: This is a hard day
t.haleem: And when Our messengers came to Lot, he was anxious for them, feeling powerless to protect them, and said, ‘This is a truly terrible day!’
t.maariful: When Our emissaries (angels) came to LūT, he was saddened because of them, and his heart felt uneasy for their sake, and He said, “This is a very hard day.”
t.khattab: When Our messenger-angels came to Lot, he was distressed and worried by their arrival. He said, “This is a terrible day.”
t.ghali: And as soon as Our Messengers came to Lut, he was vexed on their account, and he was straitened for them (i.e., He felt unable to protect them) and said, "This is a most critical day
t.irving: When Our messengers came to Lot, he was uneasy concerning them and felt helpless about warding off anything they might do, He said: "This is a critical day
t.iwq: And when Our messengers, the angels, came to Lot, he was anguished for them and felt for them great discomfort and said, "This is a trying day

t_id.kemenag: Dan tatkala datang utusan-utusan Kami (para malaikat) itu kepada Luth, dia merasa susah dan merasa sempit dadanya karena kedatangan mereka, dan dia berkata: "Ini adalah hari yang amat sulit".
t_id.sabiq: Ketika para malaikat utusan Kami datang kepada Nabi Lûth dalam bentuk pemuda-pemuda tampan, Lûth merasa susah dan menderita. Ia merasa tidak dapat melindungi mereka dan merasa susah dengan keberadaan mereka karena takut perilaku jelek kaumnya akan tertuju kepada mereka. Ia berkata, "Ini adalah hari yang teramat sulit."
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Dan tatkala datang utusan-utusan Kami itu kepada Luth, dia merasa susah) merasa repot dengan kedatangan mereka itu (dan merasa sempit dadanya karena kedatangan mereka) mereka datang dalam rupa yang tampan menyamar sebagai tamu-tamu. Hal inilah yang membuat Nabi Luth merasa takut terhadap kaumnya yang tentunya akan berlaku tidak senonoh terhadap tamu-tamunya ini (dan dia berkata, "Ini adalah hari yang amat berat.") hari yang sangat keras.
t_id.iwq: Dan tatkala datang utusan-utusan Kami (para malaikat) itu kepada Luth, dia merasa susah dan merasa sempit dadanya karena kedatangan mereka, dan dia berkata: "Ini adalah hari yang amat sulit".

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «ولما جاءت رسلنا لوطا سيء بهم» حزن بسبهم «وضاق بهم ذرعا» صدرا لأنهم حسان الوجوه في صورة أضياف فخاف عليهم قومه «وقال هذا يوم عصيب» شديد
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَلَمَّا جَآءَتۡ رُسُلُنَا لُوطٗا سِيٓءَ بِهِمۡ وَضَاقَ بِهِمۡ ذَرۡعٗا وَقَالَ هَٰذَا يَوۡمٌ عَصِيبٞ (سِيءَ بِهِمْ: سَاءَهُ مَجِيئُهُمْ, وَضَاقَ بِهِمْ ذَرْعًا: ضَاقَ صَدْرُهْ، وَاغْتَمَّ لِمَجِيئِهِمْ؛ خَوْفًا عَلَيْهِمْ مِنْ قَوْمِهِ, عَصِيبٌ: شَدِيدٌ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Dan tatkala datang utusan-utusan Kami itu kepada Luth, dia merasa susah) merasa repot dengan kedatangan mereka itu (dan merasa sempit dadanya karena kedatangan mereka) mereka datang dalam rupa yang tampan menyamar sebagai tamu-tamu. Hal inilah yang membuat Nabi Luth merasa takut terhadap kaumnya yang tentunya akan berlaku tidak senonoh terhadap tamu-tamunya ini (dan dia berkata, "Ini adalah hari yang amat berat.") hari yang sangat keras

ar: وَجَاۤءَهُۥ قَوۡمُهُۥ یُهۡرَعُونَ إِلَیۡهِ وَمِن قَبۡلُ كَانُوا۟ یَعۡمَلُونَ ٱلسَّیِّءَاتِۚ قَالَ یَـٰقَوۡمِ هَـٰۤؤُلَاۤءِ بَنَاتِی هُنَّ أَطۡهَرُ لَكُمۡۖ فَٱتَّقُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ وَلَا تُخۡزُونِ فِی ضَیۡفِیۤۖ أَلَیۡسَ مِنكُمۡ رَجُلࣱ رَّشِیدࣱ
ar_simple: وَجَاءَهُ قَوْمُهُ يُهْرَعُونَ إِلَيْهِ وَمِنْ قَبْلُ كَانُوا يَعْمَلُونَ السَّيِّئَاتِ ۚ قَالَ يَا قَوْمِ هَٰؤُلَاءِ بَنَاتِي هُنَّ أَطْهَرُ لَكُمْ ۖ فَاتَّقُوا اللَّهَ وَلَا تُخْزُونِ فِي ضَيْفِي ۖ أَلَيْسَ مِنْكُمْ رَجُلٌ رَشِيدٌ
transliteration: wajāahu qawmuhu yuh'raʿūna ilayhi wamin qablu kānū yaʿmalūna l-sayiāti qāla yāqawmi hāulāi banātī hunna aṭharu lakum fa-ittaqū l-laha walā tukh'zūni fī ḍayfī alaysa minkum rajulun rashīdun

t.sahih-int: And his people came hastening to him, and before [this] they had been doing evil deeds. He said, "O my people, these are my daughters; they are purer for you. So fear Allah and do not disgrace me concerning my guests. Is there not among you a man of reason
t.asad: And his people came running to him, impelled towards his house [by their desire]: for they had ever been wont to commit [such], abominations. Said [Lot]: "O my people! [Take instead] these daughters of mine: they are purer for you [than men]! Be, then, conscious of God, and disgrace me not by [assaulting] my guests. Is there not among you even one right-minded man
t.yusuf-ali: And his people came rushing towards him, and they had been long in the habit of practising abominations. He said: "O my people! Here are my daughters: they are purer for you (if ye marry)! Now fear Allah, and cover me not with shame about my guests! Is there not among you a single right-minded man
t.pickthall: And his people came unto him, running towards him - and before then they used to commit abominations - He said: O my people! Here are my daughters! They are purer for you. Beware of Allah, and degrade me not in (the person of) my guests. Is there not among you any upright man
t.arberry: And his people came to him, running towards him; and erstwhile they had been doing evil deeds. He said, 'O my people, these are my daughters; they are cleaner for you. So fear God, and do not degrade me in my guests. What, is there not one man among you of a right mind
t.hilali-khan: And his people came rushing towards him, and since aforetime they used to commit crimes (sodomy, etc.), he said: "O my people! Here are my daughters (i.e. the daughters of my nation), they are purer for you (if you marry them lawfully). So fear Allah and degrade me not as regards my guests! Is there not among you a single right-minded man
t.itani: And his people came rushing towards him—they were in the habit of committing sins. He said, “O my people, these are my daughters; they are purer for you. So fear God, and do not embarrass me before my guests. Is there not one reasonable man among you?”
t.mubarakpuri: And his people came rushing towards him, and since aforetime they used to commit crimes, he said: "O my people! Here are my daughters, they are purer for you. So have Taqwa of Allah and disgrace me not with regard to my guests! Is there not among you a single right-minded man"
t.qarib: His people came running to him; they were accustomed to committing foul deeds. [Lot] said, "My people, take my daughters instead. They are purer for you [than men]. Fear God, and do not disgrace me with my guests. Is there not a single right-minded man among you
t.wahiduddin: His people, who were used to committing foul deeds, came running to him. He said, "My people, here are my daughters. They are purer for you, [if you marry] so have some fear of God and do not disgrace me before my guests. Is there not a single right-minded man among you
t.shakir: And his people came to him, (as if) rushed on towards him, and already they did evil deeds. He said: O my people! these are my daughters-- they are purer for you, so guard against (the punishment of) Allah and do not disgrace me with regard to my guests; is there not among you one right-minded man
t.haleem: His people came rushing towards him; they used to commit foul deeds. He said, ‘My people, here are my daughters.They are more wholesome for you, so have some fear of God and do not disgrace me with my guests. Is there not a single right-minded man among you?’
t.maariful: And his people came to him, rushing upon him. They were already habitual to commit evil deeds. He said, “These are my daughters. They are purer for you.So, have fear of Allah and do not disgrace me in respect of my guests. Is there no reasonable man among you?”
t.khattab: And ˹the men of˺ his people—who were used to shameful deeds—came to him rushing. He pleaded, “O my people! Here are my daughters ˹for marriage˺—they are pure for you. So fear Allah, and do not humiliate me by disrespecting my guests. Is there not ˹even˺ a single right-minded man among you?”
t.ghali: And his people came to him, hurrying to him; and earlier they had been doing odious deeds. He said, "O my people, these are my daughters; they are purer for you. So, be pious to Allah, and do not disgrace me in my guests. Is there not among you a (really) right-minded man
t.irving: His people came hurrying up to him; while just before they had been performing such evil deeds! He said: "My people, these are my daughters; they are purer for you. Heed God and do not shame me through my guests. Is there not a normal man among you
t.iwq: And his people came hastening to him, and before this they had been doing evil deeds. He said, "O my people, these are my daughters; they are purer for you. So fear Allah and do not disgrace me concerning my guests. Is there not among you a man of reason

t_id.kemenag: Dan datanglah kepadanya kaumnya dengan bergegas-gegas. Dan sejak dahulu mereka selalu melakukan perbuatan-perbuatan yang keji. Luth berkata: "Hai kaumku, inilah puteri-puteriku, mereka lebih suci bagimu, maka bertakwalah kepada Allah dan janganlah kamu mencemarkan (nama)ku terhadap tamuku ini. Tidak adakah di antaramu seorang yang berakal?"
t_id.sabiq: Setelah mengetahui tetamu Nabi Lûth itu, dengan bergegas, kaumnya datang kepada Lûth. Sebelum itu mereka terkenal sering melakukan perbuatan-perbuatan keji. Lûth berkata kepada mereka, "Hai kaumku, inilah putri-putriku, kawinilah mereka. Ini lebih suci bagi kalian daripada berbuat keji dengan laki-laki. Bertakwalah kepada Allah. Peliharalah diri kalian dari siksaan-Nya. Jangan kalian permalukan aku dengan mengganggu tamu-tamuku. Tidak adakah di antara kalian seseorang yang berakal sehat yang dapat menyelamatkan kalian dari jurang kesesatan dan mencegah kejahatan kalian?
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Datanglah kepadanya kaumnya) ketika mereka mengetahui tentang tamu-tamunya itu (dengan bergegas-gegas) dengan segera (menuju kepadanya. Dan sejak dahulu) sebelum kedatangan para tetamu itu (mereka selalu mengerjakan perbuatan-perbuatan keji) yaitu menyetubuhi anus laki-laki. (Ia pernah berkata,) yakni Nabi Luth ("Hai kaumku! Inilah putri-putriku) maka kawinilah mereka (mereka lebih suci bagi kalian, maka bertakwalah kepada Allah dan janganlah kalian membuat malu) mempermalukan diriku (terhadap tamuku ini) tamu-tamu ini. (Tidak adakah di antara kalian seorang yang berakal?") yang memerintahkan kalian berbuat kebajikan dan melarang kalian melakukan perbuatan yang mungkar.
t_id.iwq: Dan datanglah kepadanya kaumnya dengan bergegas-gegas. Dan sejak dahulu mereka selalu melakukan perbuatan-perbuatan yang keji. Luth berkata: "Hai kaumku, inilah puteri-puteriku, mereka lebih suci bagimu, maka bertakwalah kepada Allah dan janganlah kamu mencemarkan (nama)ku terhadap tamuku ini. Tidak adakah di antaramu seorang yang berakal?"

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «وجاءه قومه» لما علموا بهم «يُهرعون» يسرعون «إليه ومن قبل» قبل مجيئهم «كانوا يعملون السيئات» وهي إتيان الرجال في الأدبار «قال» لوط «يا قوم هؤلاء بناتي» فتزوجوهن «هن أطهر لكم فاتقوا الله ولا تخزون» تفضحون «في ضيفي» أضيافي «أليس منكم رجل رشيد» يأمر بالمعروف وينهى عن المنكر
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَجَآءَهُۥ قَوۡمُهُۥ يُهۡرَعُونَ إِلَيۡهِ وَمِن قَبۡلُ كَانُواْ يَعۡمَلُونَ ٱلسَّيِّـَٔاتِۚ قَالَ يَٰقَوۡمِ هَـٰٓؤُلَآءِ بَنَاتِي هُنَّ أَطۡهَرُ لَكُمۡۖ فَٱتَّقُواْ ٱللَّهَ وَلَا تُخۡزُونِ فِي ضَيۡفِيٓۖ أَلَيۡسَ مِنكُمۡ رَجُلٞ رَّشِيدٞ (يُهْرَعُونَ: يُسْرِعُونَ, وَلَا تُخْزُونِ: لَا تَفْضَحُونِي, رَّشِيدٌ: يَامُرُ بِالمَعْرُوفِ، وَيَنْهَى عَنِ المُنْكَرِ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Datanglah kepadanya kaumnya) ketika mereka mengetahui tentang tamu-tamunya itu (dengan bergegas-gegas) dengan segera (menuju kepadanya. Dan sejak dahulu) sebelum kedatangan para tetamu itu (mereka selalu mengerjakan perbuatan-perbuatan keji) yaitu menyetubuhi anus laki-laki. (Ia pernah berkata,) yakni Nabi Luth ("Hai kaumku! Inilah putri-putriku) maka kawinilah mereka (mereka lebih suci bagi kalian, maka bertakwalah kepada Allah dan janganlah kalian membuat malu) mempermalukan diriku (terhadap tamuku ini) tamu-tamu ini. (Tidak adakah di antara kalian seorang yang berakal?") yang memerintahkan kalian berbuat kebajikan dan melarang kalian melakukan perbuatan yang mungkar

ar: قَالُوا۟ لَقَدۡ عَلِمۡتَ مَا لَنَا فِی بَنَاتِكَ مِنۡ حَقࣲّ وَإِنَّكَ لَتَعۡلَمُ مَا نُرِیدُ
ar_simple: قَالُوا لَقَدْ عَلِمْتَ مَا لَنَا فِي بَنَاتِكَ مِنْ حَقٍّ وَإِنَّكَ لَتَعْلَمُ مَا نُرِيدُ
transliteration: qālū laqad ʿalim'ta mā lanā fī banātika min ḥaqqin wa-innaka lataʿlamu mā nurīdu

t.sahih-int: They said, "You have already known that we have not concerning your daughters any claim, and indeed, you know what we want
t.asad: They answered: "Thou hast always known that we have no use whatever for thy daughters; and, verily, well dost thou know what we want
t.yusuf-ali: They said: "Well dost thou know we have no need of thy daughters: indeed thou knowest quite well what we want
t.pickthall: They said: Well thou knowest that we have no right to thy daughters, and well thou knowest what we want
t.arberry: They said, 'Thou knowest we have no right to thy daughters, and thou well knowest what we desire
t.hilali-khan: They said: "Surely you know that we have neither any desire nor in need of your daughters, and indeed you know well what we want
t.itani: They said, “You know well that we have no right to your daughters, and you know well what we want.”
t.mubarakpuri: They said: "Surely, you know that we have no need of your daughters, and indeed you know well what we want!"
t.qarib: They answered, "You have always known that we have no right to your daughters. You know very well what we want
t.wahiduddin: They replied, "You know we have no need of your daughters. You know very well what we are seeking
t.shakir: They said: Certainly you know that we have no claim on your daughters, and most surely you know what we desire
t.haleem: They said, ‘You know very well that we have no right to your daughters. You know very well what we want.’
t.maariful: They said, “You know that we have no claim on your daughters, and you know well what we want.”
t.khattab: They argued, “You certainly know that we have no need for your daughters. You already know what we desire!”
t.ghali: They said, "Indeed you already know (that) in no way do we have any truthful (claim) whatever to your daughters, and surely you know indeed what we would have
t.irving: They said: "You know we have no right to your daughters. You know what we want
t.iwq: They said, "You have already known that we have not concerning your daughters any claim, and verily, you know what we want

t_id.kemenag: Mereka menjawab: "Sesungguhnya kamu telah tahu bahwa kami tidak mempunyai keinginan terhadap puteri-puterimu; dan sesungguhnya kamu tentu mengetahui apa yang sebenarnya kami kehendaki".
t_id.sabiq: Mereka berkata, "Hai Lûth, kamu tahu bahwa kami tidak pantas menikahi putri-putrimu dan sama sekali kami tidak menyukai mereka. Tentunya kamu sangat tahu apa maksud kedatangan kami kepadamu."
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Mereka menjawab, "Sesungguhnya kamu telah mengetahui bahwa kami tak mempunyai keinginan terhadap putri-putrimu itu) kami tidak membutuhkannya (dan sesungguhnya kamu tentu mengetahui apa yang sekiranya kami kehendaki.") yaitu suka menyetubuhi anus laki-laki.
t_id.iwq: Mereka menjawab: "Sesungguhnya kamu telah tahu bahwa kami tidak mempunyai keinginan terhadap puteri-puterimu; dan sesungguhnya kamu tentu mengetahui apa yang sebenarnya kami kehendaki".

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «قالوا لقد علمت ما لنا في بناتك من حق» حاجة «وإنك لتعلم ما نريد» من إتيان الرجال
tafsir.siraj-ar: قَالُواْ لَقَدۡ عَلِمۡتَ مَا لَنَا فِي بَنَاتِكَ مِنۡ حَقّٖ وَإِنَّكَ لَتَعۡلَمُ مَا نُرِيدُ (مِنْ حَقٍّ: مِنْ حَاجَةٍ، أَوْ رَغْبَةٍ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Mereka menjawab, "Sesungguhnya kamu telah mengetahui bahwa kami tak mempunyai keinginan terhadap putri-putrimu itu) kami tidak membutuhkannya (dan sesungguhnya kamu tentu mengetahui apa yang sekiranya kami kehendaki.") yaitu suka menyetubuhi anus laki-laki

ar: قَالَ لَوۡ أَنَّ لِی بِكُمۡ قُوَّةً أَوۡ ءَاوِیۤ إِلَىٰ رُكۡنࣲ شَدِیدࣲ
ar_simple: قَالَ لَوْ أَنَّ لِي بِكُمْ قُوَّةً أَوْ آوِي إِلَىٰ رُكْنٍ شَدِيدٍ
transliteration: qāla law anna lī bikum quwwatan aw āwī ilā ruk'nin shadīdin

t.sahih-int: He said, "If only I had against you some power or could take refuge in a strong support
t.asad: Exclaimed [Lot]: "Would that I had the strength to defeat you, or that I could lean upon some mightier support
t.yusuf-ali: He said: "Would that I had power to suppress you or that I could betake myself to some powerful support
t.pickthall: He said: Would that I had strength to resist you or had some strong support (among you)
t.arberry: He said, 'O would that I had power against you, or might take refuge in a strong pillar
t.hilali-khan: He said: "Would that I had strength (men) to overpower you, or that I could betake myself to some powerful support (to resist you)
t.itani: He said, “If only I had the strength to stop you, or could rely on some strong support.”
t.mubarakpuri: He said: "Would that I had strength to overpower you, or that I could betake myself to some powerful support."
t.qarib: [Lot] exclaimed, "If only I had the strength to defeat you or could rely on mightier support
t.wahiduddin: He said, "If only I had the strength to stop you or could take refuge in some powerful support
t.shakir: He said: Ah! that I had power to suppress you, rather I shall have recourse to a strong support
t.haleem: He said, ‘If only I had the strength to stop you or could rely on strong support!’
t.maariful: He said, “Would that I had some power over you, or could seek refuge in a strong support!”
t.khattab: He responded, “If only I had the strength ˹to resist you˺ or could rely on a strong supporter.”
t.ghali: He said, "If only I had power against you, or had my abode valiant support
t.irving: He said: "If only I had some power over you or could seek safety at some firm support
t.iwq: And he said, "If only I had against you some power or could take refuge in a strong support

t_id.kemenag: Luth berkata: "Seandainya aku ada mempunyai kekuatan (untuk menolakmu) atau kalau aku dapat berlindung kepada keluarga yang kuat (tentu aku lakukan)".
t_id.sabiq: Lûth berkata, "Kalaulah aku memiliki kekuatan untuk menolak kalian atau keluarga yang kuat untuk berlindung, maka sikapku terhadap kalian tentu akan berbeda. Aku pasti akan menghalangi kalian mendekati tamu-tamuku akan dan aku cegah kalian dari perbuatan dosa itu."
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Luth berkata, "Seandainya aku mempunyai kekuatan untuk menolak kalian) mempunyai kemampuan untuk menolak keinginan kalian (atau kalau aku dapat berlindung kepada keluarga yang kuat") yakni keluarga yang mau menolongku, tentu aku akan menghajar kalian atas kekurangajaran kalian itu. Maka tatkala para malaikat itu melihat hal tersebut, lalu mereka mengatakan:
t_id.iwq: Luth berkata: "Seandainya aku ada mempunyai kekuatan (untuk menolakmu) atau kalau aku dapat berlindung kepada keluarga yang kuat (tentu aku lakukan)".

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «قال لو أن لي بكم قوة» طاقة «أو آوي إلى ركن شديد» عشيرة تنصرني لبطشت بكم فلما رأت الملائكة ذلك
tafsir.siraj-ar: قَالَ لَوۡ أَنَّ لِي بِكُمۡ قُوَّةً أَوۡ ءَاوِيٓ إِلَىٰ رُكۡنٖ شَدِيدٖ
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Luth berkata, "Seandainya aku mempunyai kekuatan untuk menolak kalian) mempunyai kemampuan untuk menolak keinginan kalian (atau kalau aku dapat berlindung kepada keluarga yang kuat") yakni keluarga yang mau menolongku, tentu aku akan menghajar kalian atas kekurangajaran kalian itu. Maka tatkala para malaikat itu melihat hal tersebut, lalu mereka mengatakan

ar: قَالُوا۟ یَـٰلُوطُ إِنَّا رُسُلُ رَبِّكَ لَن یَصِلُوۤا۟ إِلَیۡكَۖ فَأَسۡرِ بِأَهۡلِكَ بِقِطۡعࣲ مِّنَ ٱلَّیۡلِ وَلَا یَلۡتَفِتۡ مِنكُمۡ أَحَدٌ إِلَّا ٱمۡرَأَتَكَۖ إِنَّهُۥ مُصِیبُهَا مَاۤ أَصَابَهُمۡۚ إِنَّ مَوۡعِدَهُمُ ٱلصُّبۡحُۚ أَلَیۡسَ ٱلصُّبۡحُ بِقَرِیبࣲ
ar_simple: قَالُوا يَا لُوطُ إِنَّا رُسُلُ رَبِّكَ لَنْ يَصِلُوا إِلَيْكَ ۖ فَأَسْرِ بِأَهْلِكَ بِقِطْعٍ مِنَ اللَّيْلِ وَلَا يَلْتَفِتْ مِنْكُمْ أَحَدٌ إِلَّا امْرَأَتَكَ ۖ إِنَّهُ مُصِيبُهَا مَا أَصَابَهُمْ ۚ إِنَّ مَوْعِدَهُمُ الصُّبْحُ ۚ أَلَيْسَ الصُّبْحُ بِقَرِيبٍ
transliteration: qālū yālūṭu innā rusulu rabbika lan yaṣilū ilayka fa-asri bi-ahlika biqiṭ'ʿin mina al-layli walā yaltafit minkum aḥadun illā im'ra-ataka innahu muṣībuhā mā aṣābahum inna mawʿidahumu l-ṣub'ḥu alaysa l-ṣub'ḥu biqarībin

t.sahih-int: The angels said, "O Lot, indeed we are messengers of your Lord; [therefore], they will never reach you. So set out with your family during a portion of the night and let not any among you look back - except your wife; indeed, she will be struck by that which strikes them. Indeed, their appointment is [for] the morning. Is not the morning near
t.asad: [Whereupon the angels] said: "O Lot! Behold, we are messengers from thy Sustainer! Never shall [thy enemies] attain to thee! Depart, then, with thy household while it is yet night, and let none of you look back; [and take with thee all thy family] with the exception of thy wife: for, behold, that which is to befall these [people of Sodom] shall befall her [as well]. Verily, their appointed time is the morning [and] is not the morning nigh
t.yusuf-ali: (The Messengers) said: "O Lut! We are Messengers from thy Lord! By no means shall they reach thee! now travel with thy family while yet a part of the night remains, and let not any of you look back: but thy wife (will remain behind): To her will happen what happens to the people. Morning is their time appointed: Is not the morning nigh
t.pickthall: (The messengers) said: O Lot! Lo! we are messengers of thy Lord; they shall not reach thee. So travel with thy people in a part of the night, and let not one of you turn round - (all) save thy wife. Lo! that which smiteth them will smite her (also). Lo! their tryst is (for) the morning. Is not the morning nigh
t.arberry: They said, 'Lot, we are messengers of thy Lord. They shall not reach thee; so set forth, thou with thy family, in a watch of the night, and let not any one of you turn round, excepting thy wife; surely she shall be smitten by that which smites them. Their promised time is the morning; is the morning not nigh
t.hilali-khan: They (Messengers) said: "O Lout (Lot)! Verily, we are the Messengers from your Lord! They shall not reach you! So travel with your family in a part of the night, and let not any of you look back, but your wife (will remain behind), verily, the punishment which will afflict them, will afflict her. Indeed, morning is their appointed time. Is not the morning near
t.itani: They said, “O Lot, we are the envoys of your Lord; they will not reach you. So set out with your family during the cover of the night, and let none of you look back, except for your wife. She will be struck by what will strike them. Their appointed time is the morning. Is not the morning near?”
t.mubarakpuri: They (messengers) said: "O Lut! Verily, we are the messengers from your Lord! They shall not reach you! So travel with your family in a part of the night, and let not any of you look back; but your wife (will remain behind), verily, the punishment which will afflict them, will afflict her. Indeed, morning is their appointed time. Is not the morning near"
t.qarib: [The angels] said, "Lot, we are Messengers from your Lord. [Your enemies] will never reach you. Leave with your household in the dead of night, and do not look back. [Take all of your family with you] with the exception of your wife. The fate which will befall [the people of Sodom] will befall her [as well]. Their appointed time is the morning. Is not the morning near
t.wahiduddin: They said, "Lot, we are your Lord's messengers. By no means shall they reach you! So depart with your family while it is yet night and let none of you look back. But your wife will suffer the fate that befalls the others. Their appointed time is the morning: is the morning not near
t.shakir: They said: O Lut! we are the apostles of your Lord; they shall by no means reach you; so remove your followers in a part of the night-- and let none of you turn back-- except your wife, for surely whatsoever befalls them shall befall her; surely their appointed time is the morning; is not the morning nigh
t.haleem: They [the messengers] said, ‘Lot, we are your Lord’s messengers. They will not reach you. Leave with your household in the dead of night, and let none of you turn back. Only your wife will suffer the fate that befalls the others. Their appointed time is the morning: is the morning not near?’
t.maariful: They (the angels) said, “O LūT, we are emissaries of your Lord. They shall never reach you. So, move with your family in a part of night, and none of you shall turn to look back; except your woman. There has to befall her whatever will befall them. Their deadline is the morning. Is it not that the morning is near?”
t.khattab: The angels said, “O Lot! We are the messengers of your Lord. They will never reach you. So travel with your family in the dark of night, and do not let any of you look back, except your wife. She will certainly suffer the fate of the others. Their appointed time is the morning. Is the morning not near?”
t.ghali: They said, "O Lut, surely we are the Messengers of your Lord. They will never get to you. So set forth with your family, in a watch of the night, and let not any of you turn round, excepting your wife; surely she will be afflicted by that which afflicts them. Surely their promised (time) is the morning; is not the morning near
t.irving: They said: "Lot, we are your Lord´s messengers. They will not overtake you, so travel with your family during part of the night and do not let anyone of you turn around except for your wife. Her fate is what will strike them [too]. Their appointment will be in the morning. Is not morning near?"´
t.iwq: The angels said, "O Lot, indeed we are messengers of your Lord; therefore, they will never reach you. So set out with your family during a portion of the night and let not any among you look back - except your wife; indeed, she will be struck by that which strikes them. Indeed, their appointment is for the morning. Is not the morning near

t_id.kemenag: Para utusan (malaikat) berkata: "Hai Luth, sesungguhnya kami adalah utusan-utusan Tuhanmu, sekali-kali mereka tidak akan dapat mengganggu kamu, sebab itu pergilah dengan membawa keluarga dan pengikut-pengikut kamu di akhir malam dan janganlah ada seorangpun di antara kamu yang tertinggal, kecuali isterimu. Sesungguhnya dia akan ditimpa azab yang menimpa mereka karena sesungguhnya saat jatuhnya azab kepada mereka ialah di waktu subuh; bukankah subuh itu sudah dekat?".
t_id.sabiq: Setelah berubah menjadi bentuk aslinya, malaikat berkata, "Hai Lûth, jangan takut dan cemas! Sesungguhnya kami adalah utusan-utusan Tuhanmu, bukan manusia seperti yang tampak menurut penglihatanmu dan kaummu. Mereka tidak akan dapat menyakitimu dan mengganggumu. Pergilah dengan membawa keluarga dan pengikut-pengikutmu di akhir malam. Keluarlah bersama mereka dari negeri ini, dan jangan sampai ada seorang pun yang tertinggal supaya tidak terkena pedihnya azab. Akan tetapi jangan kamu ikut sertakan istrimu keluar bersamamu, sebab dia harus ditimpa azab seperti yang lainnya. Sesungguhnya waktu kehancuran mereka ialah waktu subuh yang sudah dekat. Maka, kau tak perlu takut."
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Para utusan itu berkata, "Hai Luth! Sesungguhnya kami adalah utusan-utusan Rabbmu, sekali-kali mereka tidak akan dapat mengganggu kamu) dengan berlaku jahat terhadap dirimu (sebab itu bawalah pergi keluarga dan pengikut-pengikut kamu di akhir) pada bagian yang terakhir dari (malam dan janganlah ada seorang pun di antara kalian yang tertinggal) supaya ia tidak melihat kengerian azab yang menimpa mereka (kecuali istrimu) dengan dibaca rafa`, yaitu imra-atuka, berkedudukan menjadi badal dari lafal ahadin. Menurut qiraat lain dibaca nashab, yaitu imra-ataka, berkedudukan menjadi istitsna dari lafal al-ahl. Artinya janganlah engkau membawa pergi dia. (Sesungguhnya dia akan ditimpa azab yang menimpa mereka) menurut suatu pendapat dikatakan bahwa Nabi Luth tidak membawa istrinya pergi. Akan tetapi menurut pendapat yang lain dikatakan bahwa Nabi Luth membawanya pergi. Hanya saja ia menoleh ke belakang seraya mengatakan, 'Aduh kaumku!' Lalu ia ditimpa oleh batu besar sehingga matilah dia." Nabi Luth bertanya kepada utusan tentang waktu pembinasaan kaumnya, maka para utusan itu berkata. ("Sesungguhnya saat jatuhnya azab kepada mereka ialah di waktu subuh) Nabi Luth meminta, 'Aku minta supaya lebih disegerakan lagi.' Mereka menjawab ('Bukankah subuh itu sudah dekat?'")
